call_meta.persona: Former work-visa professional (ex-semiconductor/tech), now self-employed solopreneur and new mother. Career + Family motivations primary, Belonging secondary. Barriers: Production gap, Prior course skepticism, Shame cycle, Schedule constraints (newborn). Not on the inburgering track.
call_meta.industry: Previously semiconductor/tech (large Dutch employer); now social media content creation (solo founder)
participants: prospect -- former tech worker, now self-employed new mother

jtbd.job: Stop being rejected from jobs for not speaking Dutch
jtbd.desired_outcome: Land work that currently requires Dutch; remove the language barrier blocking every application
jtbd.current_approach: Applying and getting denied repeatedly; trying to build her own business instead

jtbd.job: Actually speak and understand spoken Dutch, not just do grammar
jtbd.desired_outcome: Produce and comprehend real Dutch, not pass grammar exercises she doesn't understand
jtbd.current_approach: Strong at grammar drills but frozen on speaking and listening; Duolingo gave nothing usable

jtbd.job: Be able to talk to and understand her child in Dutch
jtbd.desired_outcome: Communicate with her son as he grows up Dutch-speaking
jtbd.current_approach: None yet -- anticipating the future need

jtbd.job: Not be silently excluded at work and socially
jtbd.desired_outcome: Be included by Dutch-speaking colleagues instead of isolated
jtbd.current_approach: Endured silent exclusion on an all-Dutch team until her contract ended

pains.pain: Silent exclusion on an all-Dutch team led to isolation and job loss
pains.trigger: Hired without a Dutch requirement onto a team of older Dutch colleagues who switched dynamics; employer dropped the Dutch requirement two years ago
pains.cost: Felt increasingly isolated; contract not renewed (during pregnancy)

pains.pain: Repeatedly denied jobs for lack of Dutch despite real effort
pains.trigger: Applying for jobs in the Netherlands
pains.cost: Believes Dutch would open doors currently closed to her

pains.pain: Production gap -- can do grammar but can't speak or understand
pains.trigger: Trying to actually use Dutch beyond exercises
pains.cost: Can complete grammar correctly without comprehending the text; speaking and listening feel impossible
pains.verbatim: I can only say: I have a green apple.

pains.pain: Shame and self-blame -- believes she is simply bad at languages
pains.trigger: Comparing herself to others who seem to 'just know' the rules
pains.cost: Demoralized; assumes others have an intuition she lacks

pains.pain: Anger at unrealistic expectations and an illogical language
pains.trigger: Dutch people's reactions to her language learning; the het/de rules
pains.cost: Feels judged with no acknowledgment of how hard Dutch is
pains.verbatim: Even dutch people themselves don't know when to use het or de, how am I supposed to know.

pains.pain: No time to commit -- newborn and sleep deprivation
pains.cost: Cannot start now; knows she won't have time soon

pains.pain: Resentment of subscriptions she can't use or cancel
pains.trigger: Three business-tool subscriptions she pays for but doesn't use; cancelling and re-subscribing would cost more
pains.cost: Wasted money; wary of taking on another subscription

workflow_reality.current_process: Self-study via Duolingo with no usable result; previously took an employer-paid classroom course but quit after three classes
workflow_reality.brittleness: Classroom failed on a teacher who made her feel infantilized; Duolingo produced no usable output; now no time at all because of the baby
workarounds: Following brands on Instagram/Facebook to stay reminded they exist for later

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Duolingo
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Used it but learned nothing useful -- can only produce a single canned phrase

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Employer-provided classroom course
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Quit after three classes -- hated the teacher, felt treated like a child; refused to stay even for a certificate

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Business-tool subscriptions (general)
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Stuck paying for unused subscriptions -- shapes her wariness of subscription pricing

quotes.text: I can only say: I have a green apple.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- new mother, self-employed
quotes.context: On what Duolingo actually taught her

quotes.text: Even dutch people themselves don't know when to use het or de, how am I supposed to know.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- new mother, self-employed
quotes.context: Venting about how illogical and unfairly expected the language feels

quotes.text: She feels she is just very bad at it. She thinks other people just have an intuition for it and she does not.
quotes.speaker: interviewer (paraphrase of prospect)
quotes.context: Describing her self-blame about language learning

keyword_candidates.keyword: good at Dutch grammar but can't speak
keyword_candidates.source_context: Can complete grammar correctly without understanding; speaking and listening feel impossible

keyword_candidates.keyword: can't get a job in the Netherlands without Dutch
keyword_candidates.source_context: Repeated job denials and silent workplace exclusion tied to language

keyword_candidates.keyword: why Duolingo doesn't work for learning Dutch
keyword_candidates.source_context: Duolingo produced only a single unusable phrase

keyword_candidates.keyword: how to learn Dutch with a baby and no time
keyword_candidates.source_context: Newborn and sleep deprivation block any fixed commitment

keyword_candidates.keyword: feeling excluded at work for not speaking Dutch
keyword_candidates.source_context: Silent exclusion on an all-Dutch team
